title: quercus lineata
intro: Quercus lineata, also known as the Mexican Buckeye, is a deciduous tree native to Mexico and parts of Central America, belonging to the beech family (Fagaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Quercus lineata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used to make topical creams and salves for skin treatments.
